title: Deployment Rollouts and Rollbacks
description:
Practice the full rollout lifecycle: deploy, update the image, verify revision history, and perform a rollback — the exact workflow used in production incidents.

## How Rollouts Work

Every time you change a Deployment's pod template (image, env vars, resource limits), Kubernetes creates a new **revision**. The controller performs a rolling update: it scales up a new ReplicaSet while scaling down the old one, respecting `maxSurge` and `maxUnavailable`.

You can observe this "pass the baton" dance:
```bash
kubectl get rs -w
# Old RS: 3 → 2 → 1 → 0
# New RS: 0 → 1 → 2 → 3
```

## Revision History

```bash
kubectl rollout history deployment/my-app
# REVISION  CHANGE-CAUSE
# 1         <none>
# 2         <none>
```

Kubernetes keeps the last **10 revisions** by default (configurable via `spec.revisionHistoryLimit`). Each revision corresponds to a ReplicaSet kept in the cluster.

View a specific revision's pod template:
```bash
kubectl rollout history deployment/my-app --revision=1
```

## Triggering an Update

```bash
# Imperatively update the image
kubectl set image deployment/my-app app=nginx:1.27

# Watch progress
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-app
```

## Rollback

Rollback performs a **new rolling update** to the previous revision's pod template — it is not an instant switch.

```bash
# Rollback to the previous revision
kubectl rollout undo deployment/my-app

# Rollback to a specific revision
kubectl rollout undo deployment/my-app --to-revision=1
```

After rollback, a new revision is created in history. Revision 1 rolled back to becomes the highest revision number.

## Pause and Resume

Useful for canary-style validation mid-rollout:
```bash
kubectl rollout pause deployment/my-app
# ... run smoke tests ...
kubectl rollout resume deployment/my-app
```

## Common Pitfalls

- **`--record` is deprecated** in newer kubectl versions. Use annotations or GitOps tooling to track change-cause.
- **Rollback is not instantaneous** — it respects `maxUnavailable`. For instant switchover, use Blue/Green.
- **Revision limit**: if you set `revisionHistoryLimit: 0`, all old ReplicaSets are deleted and you cannot roll back.
- **Image pull failures during rollout**: the new RS gets stuck at 0 ready; old RS stays up. `kubectl rollout status` will block — set a timeout in CI (`--timeout=120s`).
steps: Create Deployment 'my-app' with 3 replicas using nginx:1.26
Update the image to nginx:1.27 using kubectl set image
Wait for the rollout to complete — check rollout status
Verify the current image is nginx:1.27
Check rollout history — confirm 2 revisions exist
Rollback to revision 1 using kubectl rollout undo
Verify the image reverted to nginx:1.26
hints: Use 'kubectl set image deployment/my-app app=nginx:1.27' to trigger an update
Use 'kubectl rollout status deployment/my-app' to wait for completion
Use 'kubectl rollout history deployment/my-app' to list revisions
Use 'kubectl rollout undo deployment/my-app --to-revision=1' to roll back to revision 1
Check the current image with: kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}'

editor_config.initial_code:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: nginx:1.26
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80

_solution.code_solution:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: my-app
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: my-app
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: my-app
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: app
        image: nginx:1.26
        ports:
        - containerPort: 80
_solution.validation_script:
#!/bin/bash
kubectl apply -f solution.yaml

# Wait for initial 3 ready replicas
timeout=90
elapsed=0
echo 'Waiting for initial deployment...'
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  ready=$(kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  [ "$ready" = "3" ] && break
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done
ready=$(kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$ready" != "3" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected 3 ready replicas, got $ready"
  kubectl describe deployment my-app | tail -10
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Initial deployment: 3 pods ready"

# Verify initial image is nginx:1.26
initial_image=$(kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}')
if [ "$initial_image" != "nginx:1.26" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected initial image nginx:1.26, got $initial_image"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Initial image: $initial_image"

# Update image to nginx:1.27
echo 'Updating image to nginx:1.27...'
kubectl set image deployment/my-app app=nginx:1.27

# Wait for rollout to complete
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-app --timeout=90s
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "✗ Rollout to nginx:1.27 did not complete"
  kubectl describe deployment my-app | tail -10
  exit 1
fi

# Verify updated image
updated_image=$(kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}')
if [ "$updated_image" != "nginx:1.27" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected image nginx:1.27 after update, got $updated_image"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Image updated to: $updated_image"

# Verify revision history has at least 2 revisions
revision_count=$(kubectl rollout history deployment/my-app 2>/dev/null | grep -c '^[0-9]')
if [ "$revision_count" -lt 2 ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected at least 2 revisions in history, got $revision_count"
  kubectl rollout history deployment/my-app
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Rollout history shows $revision_count revisions"

# Rollback to revision 1
echo 'Rolling back to revision 1...'
kubectl rollout undo deployment/my-app --to-revision=1

# Wait for rollback to complete
kubectl rollout status deployment/my-app --timeout=90s
if [ $? -ne 0 ]; then
  echo "✗ Rollback did not complete"
  exit 1
fi

# Verify image reverted to nginx:1.26
rolled_back_image=$(kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.spec.template.spec.containers[0].image}')
if [ "$rolled_back_image" != "nginx:1.26" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected image nginx:1.26 after rollback, got $rolled_back_image"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ Rollback successful: image is $rolled_back_image"

# Confirm all 3 pods still ready after rollback
elapsed=0
while [ $elapsed -lt $timeout ]; do
  ready=$(kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
  [ "$ready" = "3" ] && break
  sleep 3
  ((elapsed+=3))
done
ready=$(kubectl get deployment my-app -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
if [ "$ready" != "3" ]; then
  echo "✗ Expected 3 ready replicas after rollback, got $ready"
  exit 1
fi
echo "✓ All 3 pods ready after rollback"
exit 0
